books.title: Bulldog Drummond
books.author: H. C. McNeile
books.summary: A demobilized officer places a newspaper advert for adventure, and its response leads him to discover a conspiracy.

books.title: The Three Hostages
books.author: John Buchan
books.summary: A former man-of-adventure is coaxed out of comfortable retirement and into danger as he aids in the rescue of three hostages.

books.title: “We”
books.author: Charles A. Lindbergh
books.summary: Legendary aviator Charles Lindbergh gives an account of his early life, how he got into flying, and his famous transatlantic flight.

books.title: Eminent Victorians
books.author: Lytton Strachey
books.summary: Four witty and biting biographies of Victorians that take aim at the moral superiority of their subject’s time.

books.title: Just So Stories
books.author: Rudyard Kipling
books.summary: A collection of short humorous fables for children, most purporting to tell the origin of animal characteristics such as the camel’s hump.

books.title: As You Like It
books.author: William Shakespeare
books.summary: Forced to flee to the Forest of Arden, the daughter of a duke encounters a variety of characters, her banished father, and even finds love.

books.title: He Who Gets Slapped
books.author: Leonid Andreyev
books.summary: A mysterious gentleman joins a circus troupe as a clown.

books.title: The Road to Oz
books.author: L. Frank Baum
books.summary: A young girl takes a passerby down the road near her farm when they find themselves transported to another land.

books.title: The Awakening
books.author: Kate Chopin
books.summary: A woman’s dawning realisation of her situation leads to a struggle for independence.

books.title: The Portent
books.author: George MacDonald
books.summary: A young Scottish tutor, gifted with a supernatural sense, discovers a haunting connection to a somnambulant noblewoman while residing at a remote English estate.

books.title: Travel Essays
books.author: Robert Louis Stevenson
books.summary: A collection of Robert Louis Stevenson’s travel essays.

books.title: The Innocence of Father Brown
books.author: G. K. Chesterton
books.summary: Amateur detective and Catholic priest Father Brown solves mysteries in this first collection of Father Brown short stories.

books.title: Religion and the Rise of Capitalism
books.author: R. H. Tawney
books.summary: A tracing of the development of religious thought from the Middle Ages to the dawn of the Industrial Revolution.

books.title: The Age of Reason
books.author: Thomas Paine
books.summary: Paine argues against the legitimacy of the Bible and institutional religion.

books.title: Spoon River Anthology
books.author: Edgar Lee Masters
books.summary: A collection of short poems recounting the lives and deaths of the residents of a small Illinois town.

books.title: Captain Jinks, Hero
books.author: Ernest Howard Crosby
books.summary: A jingoistic military officer embarks on a tragicomic quest to become a “perfect soldier.”

books.title: Short Fiction
books.author: R. A. Lafferty
books.summary: A collection of science fiction and fantasy stories by R. A. Lafferty ordered by date of publication.

books.title: When God Laughs
books.author: Jack London
books.summary: The eleventh collection of short stories by Jack London.

books.title: Polynesian Mythology
books.author: George Grey
books.summary: A collection of Māori mythology covering the start of the universe through to the spread of Māori tribes through New Zealand’s North Island.

books.title: South!
books.author: Ernest Shackleton
books.summary: A British expedition sets off to cross Antarctica, but when their ship is wrecked they have to face a long struggle for survival.
